title: Wake Word To Intent Gate
subtitle: A voice assistant spends tiny compute first, then escalates only after stronger evidence.

nodes.label: Audio Front End
nodes.description: Microphones, VAD, denoising, echo control, and low-power buffering.

nodes.label: Wake Word Check
nodes.description: A tiny detector looks for an explicit trigger phrase.
nodes.evidence: Research digest: Why Wake Words Still Matter

nodes.label: Context Gates
nodes.description: Speaker, session, risk, device state, and privacy controls add evidence.
nodes.evidence: Research digest: The New Gate Stack

nodes.label: Intent Decision
nodes.description: The system estimates whether the speech is directed to the assistant.
nodes.evidence: Research digest: Why No Wake Word Is Not No Gate

nodes.label: Voice Agent
nodes.description: ASR, reasoning, tools, and TTS wake only after the gate passes.
nodes.evidence: Research digest: Practical Design Key Ideas

edges.label: candidate speech
edges.evidence: Audio is filtered before keyword spotting.

edges.label: explicit signal
edges.evidence: Wake phrases are strong but not the only signal.
